summary: Tested auto due_date (POST /api/admin/tasks + assign-multiple) and complete Referrals module (CRUD, tracking, application submission with referral_slug). Backend: 12/13 pytest tests pass; 1 confirmed bug — TaskAssignment Pydantic model lacks 'due_date' field, so per-assignment due_date is stripped from API responses even though DB stores it correctly. Frontend: /admin/referrals page loads, modal creates referral, public /bewerbungen/{slug} shows banner 'Du bewirbst dich über ...' and tracking endpoint is called (clicks counter incremented 6x during test).

minor.issue: Each assignment in 'assignments' array is returned with due_date=null in API response, even though the value is stored correctly in MongoDB. Root cause: models/employee.py -> class TaskAssignment is missing 'due_date: Optional[str] = None'. Pydantic strips unknown fields when Task is serialized via response_model=Task. The legacy top-level task.due_date IS returned correctly.

action_items: Add 'due_date: Optional[str] = None' to TaskAssignment in /app/backend/models/employee.py so that the per-assignment due_date is included in API responses. The data is already persisted correctly; only the response model is missing the field.
critical_code_review_comments: models/employee.py TaskAssignment is missing due_date field — silently drops persisted value from API responses.
routes/admin.py create_task uses response_model=Task; Task model has no per-assignment due_date in TaskAssignment which causes the mismatch above.
applications.py uses datetime.utcnow() (deprecated, no tz) while referrals.py uses datetime.now(timezone.utc) — minor consistency issue.
seed_data_creation: Test referrals/applications/tasks were created and cleaned up automatically inside tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_referrals_and_due_date.py. Admin login is /api/admin/login (not /api/auth/login). The only failing case is the TaskAssignment.due_date field missing in the Pydantic model — once added, test_assign_multiple_auto_due_date will pass. All referral endpoints (create, list, track, toggle, delete, submit with valid/invalid/inactive slug) work and counters increment correctly. Frontend Admin → Referral-Links tab + public /bewerbungen/{slug} banner + tracking call all verified working.
rca of the issue: The route /api/admin/tasks/{id}/assign-multiple writes assignment objects containing 'due_date' to MongoDB (confirmed via direct DB query: assignment.due_date='2026-06-05'). However, when /api/admin/tasks returns the task list, it is serialized through response_model=List[Task]; Task contains assignments: List[TaskAssignment]; and TaskAssignment (models/employee.py:36-46) has no due_date field. Pydantic strips unknown fields, so each assignment is returned with effectively missing due_date. Mitigation: add 'due_date: Optional[str] = None' to TaskAssignment.
